solution_uniqueness_guard.reason: test_fix_applied and test_update_ts_context assert the exact string 'shouldSaveDraft || isTrashAttempt' in update.ts. An alternative correct fix might use a different variable name, restructure the condition differently (e.g., extract to a helper function, use a different boolean expression like 'shouldSaveDraft || data.deletedAt'), or fix the issue at a different level. These would all fail the string-matching tests.

tests_verify_behavior_not_text.reason: The core fail-to-pass tests (test_fix_applied, test_posts_collection_has_localized_field, test_trash_config_has_localization, test_payload_types_include_localized_field, test_update_ts_context) are ALL purely text-matching via 'assert X in open(path).read()'. The ESLint and unit test runs are pass-to-pass tests that don't verify the actual bug fix behavior. No test actually executes the trash operation or verifies localized data is preserved.

test_not_tautological.reason: All fail-to-pass tests check for specific strings in files. A developer could pass them by simply inserting the literal strings into the files without implementing any actual behavioral fix. For example, adding a comment containing 'shouldSaveDraft || isTrashAttempt' to update.ts would pass test_fix_applied without fixing the bug.

behavior_in_tests.reason: The instruction describes a behavior where localized field data is lost when bulk-trashing draft documents. No test actually verifies this behavior - all tests are text-matching on file contents. The actual behavioral test (preserving localized data through trash operations) is only in the solution patch's int.spec.ts addition, not in test_outputs.py.
